blocks:
Sūrat al-Zukhruf

Makkan, its number of verses is eighty-nine verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم. (1) Ḥā, Mīm. (٢) وَالْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ. (2) By the manifest Book! (٣) إِنَّا جَعَلْناهُ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا ** (3) We have made it a Qur’ān in Arabic He swore by the Qur’ān that He made it قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا ** (a Qur’ān in Arabic), and this is among the marvels (البدائع) due to the suitability between the oath (القسم) and that which is sworn upon (المقسم عليه). لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (so that you may apply reason). So that you may understand its meanings. (٤) وَإِنَّهُ فِي أُمِّ الْكِتابِ ** (4) And indeed it is in the Mother of the Book In the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ), for it is the origin (أصل) of the heavenly Books. And أمِّ الْكِتاب was recited with a kasrah (vowel mark). لَدَيْنا لَعَلِيٌ ** (with Us, sublime indeed), lofty in status (رفيع الشّأن), حَكِيمٌ ** (wise), possessing profound wisdom (حكمة بالغة). Thus it was said.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It refers to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). فِي أُمِّ الْكِتابِ ** (in the Mother of the Book) means al-Fatihah (الفاتحة), for He is written therein in His Almighty saying: اهْدِنَا الصِّراطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ (Guide us on the straight path). He said: الصِّراطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ ** (the straight path) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and the knowledge of him (ومعرفته).

And al-Qummi narrated that which is similar in meaning (ما في معناه).

(٥) أَفَنَضْرِبُ عَنْكُمُ الذِّكْرَ صَفْحاً ** (Shall We withdraw the Reminder from you, disregarding you) Shall We neglect you? فَنَضْرِبُ عَنْكُمُ الذِّكْرَ ** (So We withdraw the Reminder from you), meaning: We repel it, and We distance it, and We turn away from you with a turning away. Al-Qummi (said): It is an interrogative (statement), meaning: Shall We leave you neglected, not arguing against you with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, or with an Imam, or with proofs (حجج)? أَنْ كُنْتُمْ قَوْماً مُسْرِفِينَ ** (because you have been an extravagant people). Because you were (لان كنتم). And إن was read with a kasrah (i.e., إن كنتم), presenting the certain (المحقّق) in the manner of the doubtful (المشكوك), in order to deem them ignorant (استجهالاً لهم).

(٦) وَكَمْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ نَبِيٍّ فِي الْأَوَّلِينَ. (6) And how many a prophet We sent among the former generations! (٧) وَما يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ إِلَّا كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (7) And there did not come to them any prophet but they used to deride him. (This is) a consolation for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding the mockery of his people. (٨) فَأَهْلَكْنا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ بَطْشاً ** (8) So We destroyed those who were mightier than them in power. Meaning, from the extravagant people (القوم المسرفين), because He turned the address away from them towards the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, informing about them. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, from Quraysh. وَمَضى مَثَلُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (8 cont.) And the precedent of the former generations has passed. And their wondrous story has preceded in the Quran. And in it is a promise for the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and a threat/warning for them with the like of what happened to the former generations (الأوّلين).

(٩) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ خَلَقَهُنَّ الْعَزِيزُ الْعَلِيمُ ** (9) If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ they will surely say, ‘The All-mighty, the All-knowing created them.’ Meaning, they acknowledged My might (عزّي) and My knowledge (علمي). And what follows is a new commencement (استيناف). (١٠) الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ مَهْداً ** (10) He who made the earth a cradle for you, and made in it ways for you, so that you may be guided. He who made the earth a cradle (مَهْداً) for you, so that you may settle upon it, and made in it ways (سُبُلاً) for you, which you traverse, so that you may be guided, so that you may be guided to your destinations or to the wisdom of the Maker by reflecting upon that. (١١) وَالَّذِي نَزَّلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً بِقَدَرٍ ** (11) He who sent down water from the sky in a measured amount, and with it We revived a dead land. Likewise, you will be brought forth. And He who sent down water from the sky in a measured amount (بِقَدَرٍ), in a quantity that benefits and does not harm, and with it We revived a dead land (بَلْدَةً مَيْتاً). So, We brought life with it to land where there was no vegetation. Likewise, you will be brought forth (تُخْرَجُونَ), you will be raised (تُنْشَرُونَ) from your graves. (١٢) وَالَّذِي خَلَقَ الْأَزْواجَ كُلَّها ** (12) He who created all the pairs, and made for you ships and cattle to ride on. And He who created all the pairs (الْأَزْواجَ كُلَّها), meaning the types (أصناف) of creatures, and made for you ships (الْفُلْكِ) and cattle (الْأَنْعامِ) to ride on, on land and at sea. (١٣) لِتَسْتَوُوا عَلى ظُهُورِهِ ثُمَّ تَذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ رَبِّكُمْ إِذَا اسْتَوَيْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (13) so that you may sit firm on their backs, then remember your Lord’s blessing when you are settled on them, and say, ‘Immaculate is He who has disposed this for us, and we could not have mastered it. so that you may sit firm on their backs, then remember your Lord’s blessing when you are settled on them, remembering it with your hearts, acknowledging it and praising Him for it, and say, ‘Immaculate is He who has disposed this for us, and we could not have mastered it (مُقْرِنِينَ),’ meaning: capable (مُطِيقِينَ). That is, we would have no power (لا طاقة لنا) over the camels, nor the ships, nor the sea, were it not that Allah disposed it for us.

(١٤) وَإِنَّا إِلى رَبِّنا لَمُنْقَلِبُونَ ** (14) and indeed we are bound to return to our Lord.’ meaning: returning (راجعون). Its connection to that is because riding is for movement (التنقل), and the greatest transfer (النقلة العظمى) is the return (الانقلاب) to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And because it is perilous (مخطر), it is incumbent upon the rider not to be heedless of it and to prepare for the meeting with Allah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): If you ride the back [of a mount], then say: الحمد لله **الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنا هذا ** [the rest of] the verse.

And from his father (peace be upon them both): If you go out into the land (برّاً), then say what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنا [the rest of] the verse. For there is no servant who says it when mounting and then falls from a camel or a beast and is afflicted by anything, by Allah's permission.

(١٥) وَجَعَلُوا لَهُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ جُزْءاً ** (15) Yet they assign to Him a part from [the number of] His servants. It was said that it is connected to His saying: وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ (And if you ask them). That is, وَجَعَلُوا لَهُ ** (And they assigned to Him), after that acknowledgment, مِنْ عِبادِهِ ** (from His servants) a child, so they said the angels are the daughters of Allah. He named it a جزء (part) because the child is a piece (بضعة*) of his parent. Al-Qummi (said) regarding His saying: وَجَعَلُوا لَهُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ جُزْءاً (Yet they assign to Him a part from [the number of] His servants). He said: Quraysh said that the angels are the daughters of Allah. إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَكَفُورٌ مُبِينٌ ** (15) Indeed man is a manifest ingrate. Manifest disbelief (الكفران*).

**(١٦) أَمِ اتَّخَذَ مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ بَناتٍ وَأَصْفاكُمْ بِالْبَنِينَ ** (16) Has He taken daughters from what He creates, and chosen sons for you?

The meaning of the hamzah in am (أم) is denial (الإنكار) and astonishment (التعجّب) at their affair, since they were not satisfied with making a part (جزء) for Him, until they made for Him, from His creatures, portions (أجزاء) more specific (أخصّ) than what was chosen for them, and the most hateful of things to them, such that if one of them was given the good news of it, his grief intensified because of it, as He said.

(١٧) وَإِذا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُمْ بِما ضَرَبَ لِلرَّحْمنِ مَثَلاً ** (17) When one of them is given the good news of what he attributes to the All-beneficent as a parallel, with what he made a likeness (شَبَهاً) for Allah. And that is because every offspring of every thing is its likeness (شِبْهُهُ) and its kind (جِنْسُهُ). ظَلَّ وَجْهُهُ مُسْوَدًّا ** his face turns dark, his face becomes utterly black (اسود في الغاية) due to the gloom (الكآبة) that afflicts him. وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ ** and he is choked with suppressed grief. his heart is filled with distress (الكَرْب).

**(١٨) أَوَمَنْ يُنَشَّؤُا فِي الْحِلْيَةِ وَهُوَ فِي الْخِصامِ غَيْرُ مُبِينٍ ** (18) What, someone who is raised in ornaments, and is indistinct in dispute?

Or do they assign to Him those who are raised in adornment (الزّينة), meaning the daughters (البنات), while he/she is in dispute (الْخِصامِ), meaning in argumentation (في المجادلة), غَيْرُ مُبِينٍ ** [He/She is] not clear in argument (للحجّة). It is said: Rarely does a woman speak with her argument except that the argument is spoken against her.

And it was read يُنَشَّأُ with al-tashdid (بالتشديد), meaning: He/She is raised (اي يربّى).

(١٩)** وَجَعَلُوا الْمَلائِكَةَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ عِبادُ الرَّحْمنِ إِناثاً ** (19) They have made the angels—who are servants of the All-beneficent—females.

Another disbelief (كفر) contained in their saying, which He denounced them for, is their making the most perfect of servants (العباد) and the most honored by Allah, the most deficient in opinion (رأياً) and the most specific in gender (صنفاً). And it was recited عند الرّحمن (with the All-beneficent), representing their closeness (زلفاهم). أَشَهِدُوا خَلْقَهُمْ ** Did they attend Allah's creation of them, so they witnessed them as females? For that is something known by observation (بالمشاهدة). And this is a declaration of their ignorance (تجهيل) and a mockery (تهكّم) of them. And it was recited ءَ أشهِدُوا خَلْقَهُمْ with a ḍammah on the hamzah after the hamzah of interrogation (همزة الاستفهام). سَتُكْتَبُ شَهادَتُهُمْ ** Their testimony, which they testified with concerning the angels, will be written down, and they will be questioned about it on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

(٢٠) وَقالُوا لَوْ شاءَ الرَّحْمنُ ما عَبَدْناهُمْ ما لَهُمْ بِذلِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ. (20) And they say, 'Had the All-beneficent wished, we would not have worshipped them.' They have no knowledge of that, and they are only guessing. (٢١) أَمْ آتَيْناهُمْ كِتاباً مِنْ قَبْلِهِ ** من قبل القرآن ينطق على صحّة ما قالوه فَهُمْ بِهِ مُسْتَمْسِكُونَ. (21) Did We give them a Book before it, so they hold fast to it? from before the Quran, which speaks to the correctness of what they said. (٢٢) بَلْ قالُوا إِنَّا وَجَدْنا آباءَنا عَلى أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلى آثارِهِمْ مُهْتَدُونَ ** أي لا حجّة لهم على ذلك من جهة العقل ولا من جهة النظر وانّما جنحوا فيه الى تقليد آبائهم الجهلة والأمّة الطريقة التي تؤمّ. (22) Rather they say, 'We found our fathers following a creed, and we are guided by their footsteps.' Meaning, they have no proof (حجّة) for that, neither from the perspective of reason (العقل) nor from the perspective of contemplation (النظر). Rather, they only inclined therein toward imitating (تقليد) their ignorant fathers. And الأمّة (al-Ummah) is the way (الطريقة) that is followed (تؤمّ).

**(٢٣) وَكَذلِكَ ما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ إِلَّا قالَ مُتْرَفُوها إِنَّا وَجَدْنا آباءَنا عَلى أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلى آثارِهِمْ مُقْتَدُونَ ** (23) Thus, We did not send any warner before you into any town but its affluent ones said, 'Indeed, we found our fathers following a creed, and we are indeed following in their footsteps.'

A consolation for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and an indication that blind following (التقليد) in matters such as this is an ancient misguidance (ضلال قديم). And in specifying the affluent (المترفين), there is an indication that indulgence (التنعم) and the love of idleness (حبّ البطالة) diverted them from examining the blind following (التقليد).

(٢٤) قالَ أَوَلَوْ جِئْتُكُمْ بِأَهْدى مِمَّا وَجَدْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ آباءَكُمْ ** (He said, 'What, even if I bring you a guidance better than what you found your fathers following?') Meaning: Will you follow your fathers even if I bring you a religion more guided than the religion of your fathers? And this is a narration of a past matter revealed to the warner (النذير), or it is an address to our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. And it was read (وقريء) as "قال" (He said), meaning the warner (النذير). قالُوا إِنَّا بِما أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ كافِرُونَ ** (They said, 'We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.') That is, even if it were more guided, [they said this] to cause despair (اقناطاً) for the warner (النذير) regarding them looking into it or reflecting upon it.

(٢٥) فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ ** So We took vengeance on them, by uprooting (بالاستيصال). ** فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** so observe how was the fate of the deniers. And do not be concerned by their denial. (٢٦) وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ ** (26) When Abraham said And remember the time of this saying of his, so that they may see how he disavowed blind imitation (التقليد) and held fast to the proof (البرهان), or so that they may imitate him if they must imitate someone, for he is the noblest of their forefathers. لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ إِنَّنِي بَراءٌ مِمَّا تَعْبُدُونَ to his father and his people, 'I am indeed absolved of what you worship, absolved of your worship or your object of worship (معبودكم), [the word بَراءٌ being] a verbal noun (مصدر) used as an adjective (نُعِتَ به).

(٢٧) إِلَّا الَّذِي فَطَرَنِي فَإِنَّهُ سَيَهْدِينِ ** (27) Except Him Who originated me, for He will certainly guide me. Guidance after guidance. (٢٨) وَجَعَلَها ** And He made it meaning the word of Monotheism (كلمة التوحيد), كَلِمَةً باقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ ** a lasting word among his posterity, among his descendants (في ذريته), so that there would always be among them one who affirms the Oneness of Allah (يوحّد الله), calls to His Oneness (توحيده), and is an Imam (إماماً) and a proof (حجّة) over the creatures (الخلائق). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ** so that they may return. Those among them who committed polytheism (أشرك) may return through the call (بدعاء) of the one who affirmed His Oneness (وحّده). وفي الإكمال عن السجّاد عليه السلام قال : فينا نزلت هذه الآية وَجَعَلَها كَلِمَةً باقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ والإمامة في عقب الحسين عليه السلام إلى يوم القيامة. In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), he said: "This Ayah was revealed concerning us: And He made it a lasting word among his posterity. And the Imamate (الإمامة) is in the progeny (عقب) of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة)." وفي العلل عن الباقر عليه السلام وفي المعاني والمناقب والمجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : مثله. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Ma'ani, al-Manaqib, and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the like of it. وفي الاحتجاج عن النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله في خطبة الغدير : معاشر النّاس القرآن يعرّفكم انّ الأئمّة عليهم السلام من بعده من ولده وعرّفتكم انّهم منّي وانا منهم حيث يقول الله عزّ وجلّ وَجَعَلَها كَلِمَةً باقِيَةً فِي عَقِبِهِ وقلت لن تضلّوا ما ان تمسّكتم بهما. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) in the Sermon of Ghadir (خطبة الغدير): "O people! The Quran informs you that the Imams (peace be upon them) after him are from his descendants (ولده), and I have informed you that they are from me and I am from them, where Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: And He made it a lasting word among his posterity. And I said: 'You shall never go astray as long as you hold fast to both of them.'" وفي المناقب : ان النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله سئل عن هذه الآية فقال الإمامة في عقب الحسين عليه السلام يخرج من صلبه تسعة من الأئمّة منه مهديّ هذه الأمّة والقمّيّ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ يعني الأئمّة عليهم السلام يَرْجِعُونَ إلى الدنيا. In al-Manaqib: The Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "The Imamate (الإمامة) is in the progeny (عقب) of al-Husayn (peace be upon him). Nine Imams (الأئمّة) will emerge from his loins, including the Mahdi (مهديّ) of this nation." And al-Qummi [said]: so that they may return, meaning the Imams (peace be upon them), they may return to the world (الدنيا).

(٢٩) بَلْ مَتَّعْتُ هؤُلاءِ وَآباءَهُمْ ** (29) Rather, I provided these and their fathers with comforts These are those contemporary with the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from Quraysh. وَآباءَهُمْ ** and their fathers by extending their lifespan and granting them blessings, so they became deluded by that and plunged into desires. حَتَّى جاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ وَرَسُولٌ مُبِينٌ. until the truth came to them and a manifest apostle.

(٣٠) وَلَمَّا جاءَهُمُ الْحَقُ ** (30) And when the truth came to them لينبّههم عن غفلتهم قالُوا هذا سِحْرٌ وَإِنَّا بِهِ كافِرُونَ ** to awaken them from their heedlessness (30) they said, 'This is a magic, and we are disbelievers in it.' ضمّوا الى شركهم معاندة الحقّ والاستخفاف به. They added to their polytheism (شركهم) the antagonism toward the truth (معاندة الحقّ) and contempt for it (الاستخفاف به). (٣١) وَقالُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ ** (31) And they said, 'Why was not this Qur’ān sent down to a man from the two cities من إحدى القريتين بمكّة والطائف عَظِيمٍ ** from one of the two cities, Mecca (مكّة) and Ta'if (الطائف) (31) great بالجاه والمال كالوليد بن مغيرة بمكّة وعروة بن مسعود الثقفي بالطائف فانّ الرسالة منصب عظيم لا يليق الا بعظيم ولم يعلموا انّها رتبة روحانيّة تستدعي عظيم النفس بالتحلّي بالفضائل والكمالات القدسيّة لا التزخرف بالزّخارف الدنيويّة. in status (بالجاه) and wealth (والمال), such as al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah (الوليد بن مغيرة) in Mecca (بمكّة) and 'Urwah ibn Mas'ud al-Thaqafi (عروة بن مسعود الثقفي) in Ta'if (بالطائف), for the Messengership (الرسالة) is a great position (منصب عظيم) that is not fitting except for a great man (عظيم). But they did not know that it is a spiritual rank (رتبة روحانيّة) that necessitates greatness of soul (عظيم النفس) through adornment with virtues (بالفضائل) and sacred perfections (الكمالات القدسيّة), not through ornamentation with worldly embellishments (الزّخارف الدنيويّة).

(٣٢) أَهُمْ يَقْسِمُونَ رَحْمَتَ رَبِّكَ ** (32) Are they the ones who dispense the mercy of your Lord? This is a denial (إنكار) containing an imputation of ignorance (تجهيل) and astonishment (تعجيب) at their arbitrary judgment (تحكّمهم). What is meant by "the mercy" (الرحمة) is Prophethood (النبوّة). نَحْنُ قَسَمْنا بَيْنَهُمْ مَعِيشَتَهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** We have apportioned their livelihood among them in the life of the world, even though they are incapable of managing it themselves. وَرَفَعْنا بَعْضَهُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجاتٍ ** and raised some of them above others in ranks, and We caused disparity (التفاوت) between them in provision (الرزق) and other matters. لِيَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضاً سُخْرِيًّا ** so that some of them may take others into service. so that some of them may employ others in their needs, resulting in affection (تألّف) and mutual support (تضامن) between them, and thereby the order of the world (نظام العالم) is maintained—not due to any perfection in the one whose provision is ample (الموسع) nor due to any deficiency in the one whose provision is restricted (المقتر). Furthermore, they have no right to object to Us concerning this, nor any authority to interfere (تصرّف). So how can they interfere in that which is superior to this? And the mercy of your Lord—meaning this Prophethood (النبوّة) and what follows it—is better than what they amass of the debris (حطام) of the world, and the great provision (رزق) that comes from it, not from Him (Allah).

In al-Ihtijaj and in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), narrated from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: One day, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was sitting in the courtyard of the Ka'bah when a group of the chiefs of Quraysh gathered. He then related the hadith as previously mentioned in Surat Bani Isra'il, until he said: 'Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah said to him: "Had Allah intended to send a messenger to us, He would have sent the most esteemed among us in wealth and the best in status. Why was this Qur'an, which you claim Allah sent down to you and appointed you as a messenger with, not sent down عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ ** (upon a great man from the two cities)?" meaning either Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah in Mecca or 'Urwah ibn Mas'ud al-Thaqafi in Al-Ta'if. Then he mentioned something, until the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "As for your saying: لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ ** (Why was this Qur’an not sent down upon a great man from the two cities?)—Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah in Mecca or 'Urwah in Al-Ta'if—Allah does not regard the wealth of this world as great as you regard it, nor does it hold any significance (خطر) with Him as it holds with you. Indeed, if the world were worth the wing of a gnat to Him, He would not give a disbeliever who opposes Him a single drink of water. The division of Allah is not up to you; rather, Allah is the Distributor of Mercies (الرحمات) and the Doer of whatever He wills among His male and female servants. He, the Mighty and Majestic, is not one who fears anyone, as you fear someone due to his wealth and status, so that He would grant him Prophethood (النبوّة) for that reason. Nor is He one who covets anyone's wealth or status, as you covet, so that He would single him out for Prophethood for that reason. Nor is He one who loves anyone out of caprice (محبّة الهوى), as you love, thereby advancing one who does not deserve advancement. Rather, His dealing is based on justice (العدل). He does not prefer for the most excellent ranks of the religion and its majesty (جلاله) except the most excellent in obedience to Him and the most glorious in His service. Likewise, He does not delay anyone in the ranks of the religion and its majesty except the one who is slowest in obedience to Him. Since this is His attribute, He does not look at wealth or status. Rather, this wealth and status are from His favor (تفضّله), and none of His servants has a binding claim (ضربة لازب) upon Him. Thus, it cannot be said to Him: 'If You favor a servant with wealth, You must also favor him with Prophethood,' because no one can compel Him to act contrary to His Will, nor can anyone impose a favor upon Him, since He bestowed a favor before that (the initial grace). Do you not see, O 'Abdullah, how He enriched one person and made his appearance ugly, and how He beautified the appearance of another and impoverished him? And how He honored one person and impoverished him, and how He enriched one person and made him lowly? Furthermore, this rich person cannot say: 'Why was the beauty of so-and-so not added to my wealth (يساري)?' Nor can the beautiful person say: 'Why was the wealth of so-and-so not added to my beauty?' Nor can the honorable person say: 'Why was the wealth of so-and-so not added to my honor?' Nor can the lowly person say: 'Why was the honor of so-and-so not added to my lowliness (ضعتي)?' Rather, the judgment belongs to Allah; He apportions as He wills and acts as He wills, and He is Wise in His actions and Praised in His deeds. And that is His saying: وَقالُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ ** (And they say, 'Why was this Qur’an not sent down upon a great man from the two cities?') Allah the Almighty said: أَهُمْ يَقْسِمُونَ رَحْمَتَ رَبِّكَ ** (Are they the ones who dispense the mercy of your Lord?) O Muhammad, نَحْنُ قَسَمْنا بَيْنَهُمْ (We have apportioned their livelihood among them) مَعِيشَتَهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (in the life of the world), so We made some dependent on others. We made this one dependent on the wealth of that one, and We made that one dependent on the commodity of this one and on his service. Thus, you see the most glorious of kings and the richest of the wealthy in need of the poorest of the poor in some way: either a commodity that the poor person possesses but the king does not, or a service that the poor person is fit for, without which it is impossible for that king to dispense, or a branch of knowledge and wisdom (العلوم والحكم) that the king is poor and needs to benefit from that poor person. So, this poor person needs the wealth of that rich king, and that king needs the knowledge, opinion, or understanding (معرفته) of this poor person. Furthermore, the king cannot say: 'Why was the knowledge of this poor person not combined with my wealth?' Nor can the poor person say: 'Why was the wealth of this rich king not combined with my opinion, my knowledge, and the various kinds of wisdom (فنون الحكم) I possess?'"

(33) وَلَوْ لا أَنْ يَكُونَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (33) (And were it not that mankind would become one community) Were it not that they would desire disbelief if they saw the disbelievers in abundance and luxury, due to their love for the world (الدُّنْيَا), and thus gather upon it (disbelief), لَجَعَلْنا لِمَنْ يَكْفُرُ بِالرَّحْمنِ لِبُيُوتِهِمْ سُقُفاً مِنْ فِضَّةٍ وَمَعارِجَ ** (We would have made for those who disbelieve in the All-beneficent, for their houses, roofs of silver and stairways) meaning, ascenders (وَمَصَاعِدَ), عَلَيْها يَظْهَرُونَ ** (by which they ascend) they ascend the roofs (السُّطُوح). And سُقُفاً was recited in the singular form (مُفْرَداً).

(٣٤) وَلِبُيُوتِهِمْ أَبْواباً وَسُرُراً عَلَيْها يَتَّكِؤُنَ ** (34) and doors for their houses, and couches on which they recline. Meaning أَبْواباً وَسُرُراً ** (doors and couches) made of silver. (٣٥) وَزُخْرُفاً ** (35) and ornaments. And adornment (زينة). Al-Qummi (said regarding) أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (a single nation): meaning, upon a single doctrine (مذهب واحد). And regarding وَزُخْرُفاً ** (and ornaments), he said: The house adorned (المزخرف) with gold. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If Allah had done that to them (the people), no one would have believed. But He placed rich people among the believers and poor people among the disbelievers, and He placed poor people among the believers and rich people among the disbelievers. Then He tested them through command (الأمر), prohibition (النهي), patience (الصبر), and contentment (الرضا). In al-Kafi and al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: By this, He meant the nation (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that they would be upon a single religion (دين واحد), all of them disbelievers (كفّاراً). If Allah had done that to the nation (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the believers would have grieved and been distressed by it, and they would not have intermarried with them nor inherited from them. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "Were it not that My believing servant would find distress within himself, I would surely bind the disbeliever with a band (عصابة) of gold." وَإِنْ ** (And indeed) meaning, and indeed كُلُّ ذلِكَ لَمَّا مَتاعُ الْحَياةِ (35) All that is but the wares of the life الدُّنْيا ** (of the world). And it was read (وقرئ) lamma (لمّا) with tashdid (بالتشديد), meaning illa (الّا), in which case in (إن) is negative (نافية). وَالْآخِرَةُ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ. (35) and the Hereafter is with your Lord for the Godwary. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, Glorious is His praise, apologizes to His believing servant who is in need (المحوج) in this world, just as a brother apologizes to his brother. He says: "By My Might (وعزّتي), I did not make you needy in this world out of any contempt (هوانٍ) for you on My part. Lift this curtain (السّجف) and look at what I have compensated you with in the world." He (the Imam) said: So he lifts it and says: "What harm did what You withheld from me cause me, alongside what You compensated me with?" I say: Al-Sijf (السّجف), with an undotted sin (المهملة) and jim (الجيم), means the curtain (الستر). And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O company of the poor (المساكين), be cheerful in soul (طيبوا نفساً) and give Allah contentment (الرّضا) from your hearts, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will reward you for your poverty. But if you do not do so, there is no reward for you." And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: There was no believer among the descendants of Adam (peace be upon him) except that he was poor, nor any disbeliever except that he was rich, until Ibrahim (peace be upon him) came and said: رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا فِتْنَةً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (65) Our Lord! Do not make us a trial for the faithless. So Allah placed wealth (أموالاً) and need (حاجة) among these (the believers), and wealth (أموالاً) and need (حاجة) among those (the disbelievers).

(٣٦) وَمَنْ يَعْشُ عَنْ ذِكْرِ الرَّحْمنِ ** (36) Whoever is willfully blind to the remembrance of the All-beneficent becomes willfully blind and turns away from it due to his excessive preoccupation with sensory things (المحسوسات) and his immersion in desires (الشهوات). نُقَيِّضْ ** We cause and We decree لَهُ شَيْطاناً فَهُوَ لَهُ قَرِينٌ ** who whispers to him and misleads him constantly. وقرئ يقيّض (with the ياء). In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Whoever undertakes sin (الإثم) becomes willfully blind (أعشى) to the remembrance of Allah, the Almighty. And whoever abandons taking (guidance) from the one whose obedience Allah has commanded, a devil is assigned to him فَهُوَ لَهُ قَرِينٌ.** (who is his companion.)

(٣٧) وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ** (37) And indeed they bar them from the way, And indeed the devils (الشياطين) bar the blind (العاشين) from the path (الطريق) which ought to be followed (يُسْبَل). وَيَحْسَبُونَ ** And they suppose—meaning the blind (العاشون)— أَنَّهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ. (37) and they suppose that they are guided. (٣٨) حَتَّى إِذا جاءَنا ** (38) When he comes to Us, Meaning the blind one (العاشي). And it was read (جَاءَانَا) in the dual form, meaning the blind one (العاشي) and the devil (الشيطان). قالَ ** The blind one (العاشي) will say to the devil (الشيطان): يا لَيْتَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ ** (38) he will say, 'I wish there had been between me and you the distance of the two Easts!' The distance of the East from the West (بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقِ مِنَ الْمَغْرِبِ). فَبِئْسَ الْقَرِينُ ** (38) What an evil companion! —you are.

(٣٩) وَلَنْ يَنْفَعَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ ** (39) And it will not benefit you today, — what you are upon of wishing — إِذْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ فِي الْعَذابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ.** since you did wrong, that you are partners in the punishment.

al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): These two verses were revealed thus: حَتَّى إِذا جائانا (until when they come to Us) — meaning so-and-so and so-and-so — one of them says to his companion when he sees him: يا لَيْتَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ فَبِئْسَ الْقَرِينُ ** (O, would that there had been between me and you the distance of the two Easts! What an evil companion!) Then Allah said to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said to so-and-so and so-and-so and their followers: لَنْ يَنْفَعَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ إِذْ ظَلَمْتُمْ ** (It will not benefit you today, since you did wrong) — [meaning] the Family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them) their right — أَنَّكُمْ فِي الْعَذابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ. that you are partners in the punishment.

(40) أَفَأَنْتَ تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ أَوْ تَهْدِي الْعُمْيَ ** (40) Are you going to make the deaf hear, or guide the blind, A denial mixed with astonishment that he could be the one capable of guiding them, after their persistence in disbelief and their immersion in error, such that their weak sight (عشاهم) became blindness (عمىً) coupled with deafness (الصّمم). وَمَنْ كانَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ (and him who is in manifest error?) is a conjunction upon الْعُمْيَ ** considering the difference between the two descriptions. And in it is an indication that the cause for that is their firm establishment in an error that is not hidden.

(٤١) فَإِمَّا نَذْهَبَنَّ بِكَ ** (41) If We take you away, meaning: If We seize your soul before He grants you victory over them through their punishment, and the mā (ما) is extra (مزيدَة) for emphasis, فَإِنَّا مِنْهُمْ مُنْتَقِمُونَ ** We shall indeed take vengeance on them after you.

(٤٢) أَوْ نُرِيَنَّكَ الَّذِي وَعَدْناهُمْ ** (42) or show you what We have promised them, or if We intend to show you what وَعَدْناهُمْ ** We have promised them of the punishment, **فَإِنَّا عَلَيْهِمْ مُقْتَدِرُونَ ** We are indeed powerful over them. They will not escape Us.

In al-Majma' (al-Bayan): It is narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was shown what his progeny (ذُرِّيَّتَهُ) would face from his community after him, and he remained withdrawn (مُنْقَبِضاً) and did not relax, laughing, until He met Allah the Almighty. He said: And Jābir ibn ʿAbd Allāh al-Ansārī narrated: I was the closest of them to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم during the Farewell Pilgrimage (حِجَّة الوَدَاع) in Minā, until he said: "I shall not find you returning after me as disbelievers (كُفَّاراً), striking the necks of one another. By Allah, if you do this, you will recognize me in the battalion (الْكَتِيبَة) that fights you." Then he turned behind him and said: "Or ʿAlī," three times. We realized that Gabriel (جبرئيل) nudged him, and immediately following that, Allah sent down: فَإِمَّا نَذْهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنْهُمْ مُنْتَقِمُونَ (If We take you away, We shall indeed take vengeance on them) through ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib.

I say: That will only occur during the Return (الرَّجْعَة).

And al-Qummī narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him) that He said: فَإِمَّا نَذْهَبَنَّ بِكَ ** O Muḥammad, from Mecca (مَكَّة) to Medina (الْمَدِينَة), We shall indeed return you to it and take vengeance on them through ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (peace be upon him). Other narrations concerning this meaning have already preceded in Sūrat al-Mu’minūn.

(٤٣) فَاسْتَمْسِكْ بِالَّذِي أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. (43) Hold fast to what has been revealed to you; indeed, you are on a straight path. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): إِنَّكَ عَلى ** (Indeed, you are on) the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him), and Ali is the Straight Path (الصراط المستقيم). (٤٤) وَإِنَّهُ لَذِكْرٌ لَكَ وَلِقَوْمِكَ وَسَوْفَ تُسْئَلُونَ. (44) And indeed, it is a reminder for you and your people, and soon you will be questioned. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We are his people (قومه), and we are the ones who will be questioned (المسئولون)." And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "He meant us, and we are the People of the Remembrance (أهل الذكر), and we are the ones who will be questioned (المسئولون)." And from him (peace be upon him): "The Remembrance (الذكر) is the Quran, and we are his people (قومه), and we are the ones who will be questioned (المسئولون)." And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his Household (أهل بيته) are the People of the Remembrance (أهل الذكر), and they are the ones who will be questioned (المسئولون)."

(٤٥) **وَسْئَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنا أَجَعَلْنا مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمنِ آلِهَةً يُعْبَدُونَ ** (45) And ask those of Our apostles whom We sent before you, whether We have appointed any gods besides the All-beneficent to be worshipped.

Did We decree the worship of idols, and did it come in any religion (مِلَّة) of their religions (مِلَلِهِم).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse: "Who is the one whom Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله asked, when there were five hundred years between him and Jesus?" So, He (peace be upon him) recited this verse: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي أَسْرى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلاً مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى الَّذِي بارَكْنا حَوْلَهُ لِنُرِيَهُ مِنْ آياتِنا (Immaculate is He Who carried His servant on a night journey from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose environs We have blessed, that We might show him some of Our signs). He (peace be upon him) said: "It was among the signs (الآيات) that Allah showed Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله when He carried him on the night journey to the Holy House (البيت المقدّس) that Allah gathered for him the first and the last of the Prophets (النبيّين) and the Messengers (المرسلين). Then He commanded Gabriel (جبرئيل) to call the adhan in pairs (شفعاً) and the iqamah in pairs (شفعاً). Then, in his iqamah, he said: حيّ على خير العمل (Hasten to the best of deeds). Then Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله stepped forward and led the people in prayer. Then Allah revealed to Him: وَسْئَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنا (And ask those of Our apostles whom We sent) [the rest of] the verse. So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: 'To what do you bear witness, and what were you worshipping?' They replied: 'We bear witness that there is no god except Allah, alone, without any partner, and that you are indeed the Messenger of Allah (ص). Our covenants (مواثيقنا) and pledges (عهودنا) were taken upon this.'"

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition (حديث): "As for His saying: وَسْئَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنا , this is among the proofs (براهين) of our Prophet which Allah granted Him, and by which He made the argument (الحجّة) binding upon the rest of His creation. Because when He sealed the Prophets with him, and Allah made Him a Messenger to all nations (الأمم) and the rest of the religions (الملل), He specifically honored Him with the ascent (الارتقاء) to the heaven during the Mi'raj (Ascension). On that day, He gathered the Prophets for Him, so He learned from them what they were sent with and what they carried of the firm resolutions (عزائم) of Allah, His signs (آياته), and His proofs (براهينه). So, they all acknowledged His virtue (فضله), and the virtue of the Successors (الأوصياء) and the Proofs (الحجج) on earth after Him, and the virtue of the Shi'a of His Successor, from among the believing men and believing women, who submitted to the people of virtue regarding their virtue and did not show arrogance concerning their command. And He knew who among their nations obeyed them and who disobeyed them, and the rest of those who passed away and those who remained, or those who preceded and those who came later. A parallel to these two narrations has previously appeared in Surat Yunus (peace be upon him)."

(٤٦) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مُوسى بِآياتِنا إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ فَقالَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (46) Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his elite. He said, 'I am the apostle of the Lord of all the worlds.' (٤٧) فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ بِآياتِنا إِذا هُمْ مِنْها يَضْحَكُونَ They mocked them the moment they saw them and did not reflect upon them. (47) But when he brought them Our signs, behold, they laughed at them. (٤٨) وَما نُرِيهِمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ إِلَّا هِيَ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ أُخْتِها وَأَخَذْناهُمْ بِالْعَذابِ like the years (كالسنين), the flood (والطوفان), and the locusts (والجراد) لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ. (48) We did not show them any sign but that it was greater than its counterpart, and We seized them with the punishment so that they might return. (٤٩) وَقالُوا يا أَيُّهَا السَّاحِرُ It was said they called him that in that state due to the severity of their obstinacy (شكيمتهم) and the excess of their foolishness (حماقتهم), or because they used to call the brilliant scholar (العالم الباهر) a magician (ساحراً). And al-Qummi (said it means): O scholar (يا أيّها العالم) ادْعُ لَنا رَبَّكَ بِما عَهِدَ عِنْدَكَ ** (meaning) that He remove the punishment from us إِنَّنا لَمُهْتَدُونَ.** (49) They said, 'O magician! Invoke your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you. We will indeed be guided.'

(٥٠) فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذابَ إِذا هُمْ يَنْكُثُونَ ** (50) But when We removed the scourge from them, behold, they broke their promise. [They broke] their covenant of being guided (بالاهتداء). (٥١) وَنادى فِرْعَوْنُ فِي قَوْمِهِ ** (51) Pharaoh proclaimed among his people, [He proclaimed] in their assembly and among themselves, after the removal of the torment from them, fearing that some of them might believe. قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَلَيْسَ لِي مُلْكُ مِصْرَ وَهذِهِ الْأَنْهارُ ** saying, 'O my people! Is not the kingdom of Egypt mine, and these rivers [These are] the Nile, and most of them were four. تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِي أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ **ذلك. that run at my feet? Do you not perceive?' [Do you not perceive] that.

(٥٢) أَمْ أَنَا خَيْرٌ ** (52) Am I not better with this dominion (المسلكة) and abundance (البسطة) مِنْ هذَا الَّذِي هُوَ مَهِينٌ ** (than this one who is contemptible) weak, contemptible, not fit for leadership (الرئاسة) وَلا يَكادُ يُبِينُ ** (and can scarcely speak clearly?) speech by him due to the impediment (الرتّة) (1). So how is he fit for the Messengership (الرّسالة)? And the particle أم (am) is either disjunctive (منقطعة), and the hamzah (interrogative) within it is for affirmation (التقرير), or it is conjunctive (متّصلة), and the meaning is: أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ ** (Do you not perceive?) so you know that I am better than him.

footnote (١) *الرتّة* (*al-Rattah*), with a *ḍammah* (vowel point), means inarticulateness (*العجمة*).

(٥٣) فَلَوْ لا أُلْقِيَ عَلَيْهِ أَسْوِرَةٌ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ ** (53) Why have not bracelets of gold been cast upon him? Meaning: Why were not the keys (مقاليد) of kingship cast upon him if he was truthful? Since they used to, when they appointed a man as chief (سوّدوا), adorn him with bracelets (سوّروه) and encircle him with a collar (طوّقوه) of gold. And أَسْوِرَةٌ ** is the plural of أسوار (aswār), meaning the bracelet (السوار). And it was recited (وقرئ) as اسورة. **أَوْ جاءَ مَعَهُ الْمَلائِكَةُ مُقْتَرِنِينَ ** or angels come along with him in attendance? Attending, assisting him or confirming him.

(٥٤) فَاسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُ ** (54) So he fooled his people He made their intellects (أحلامهم) light (foolish), or he sought from them lightness (الخفّة) in obeying him, and he called them, فَأَطاعُوهُ (so they obeyed him) in what he commanded them. إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْماً فاسِقِينَ ** (Indeed, they were a wicked people.) They obeyed that wicked one (الفاسق). In Nahj al-Balaghah: And indeed, Moses son of Imran (peace be upon him), and his brother Aaron (peace be upon him) entered upon Pharaoh, and upon them were woolen cloaks (مدارع الصوف), and in their hands were staffs (العصا). So they stipulated to him that if he submitted (أسلم), his kingdom would remain and his glory (عزّه) would endure. So he said: "Are you not astonished by these two? They stipulate for me the endurance of glory and the permanence of the kingdom, while they are in the state of poverty (الفقر) and humiliation (الذلّ) that you see!" "Why were bracelets of gold not cast upon them?"—this was to magnify gold and its accumulation, and to belittle wool and its wearing. And had Allah, the Glorified, willed for His prophets, when He sent them, to open for them the treasures of gold (الذّهبان), the mines of precious stones (العقيان), and the plantings of gardens (الجنان), and to gather with them the birds of the sky and the beasts of the earth, He would have done so. And had He done so, the trial (البلاء) would have ceased, the recompense (الجزاء) would have been nullified, the tidings (الأنباء) would have vanished, the wages of the afflicted (المبتلين) would not have been due to those who spoke (the truth), the believers would not have deserved the reward of the virtuous (المحسنين), and the names (الأسماء) would not have necessitated their meanings (معانيها). But Allah, the Glorified, made His messengers possessors of strength (قوّة) in their resolve (عزائمهم), and weakness (ضعفة) in what the eyes see of their conditions, [this is] along with contentment (قناعة) that fills hearts and eyes with richness (غنى), and need (خصاصة) that fills sights and hearings with harm (أذى). And if the prophets had been people of strength (قوّة) that is not sought after, and glory (عزّة) that is not oppressed, and a kingdom toward which the necks of men are stretched and for which the knots of saddles (عقد الرّحال) are tied, that would have been easier for the creation in consideration (الاعتبار) and further removed for them from arrogance (الاستكبار). And they would have been safe from a fear (رهبة) that overpowers them or a desire (رغبة) that inclines them, and the evil deeds (السيئات) would have been shared, and the good deeds (الحسنات) divided. But Allah, the Glorified, willed that the following of His messengers, the believing in His Books, the humility (الخشوع) before His Face, the submission (الاستكانة) to His command, and the surrender (الاستسلام) to His obedience be matters exclusive to Him, unmixed by any impurity (شائبة) from anything else. And whenever the affliction (البلوى) and the test (الاختبار) were greater, the reward (المثوبة) and the recompense (الجزاء) were more abundant.

(55) فَلَمَّا آسَفُونا انْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْناهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** (55) So when they provoked Our wrath, We took vengeance on them, drowning them all. They angered Us through excessive obstinacy (العناد) and disobedience (العصيان). We took vengeance on them, drowning them all in the sea (اليمّ).

In al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that he said concerning this Ayah: Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, does not grieve (يأسف) as we grieve (كأسفنا). Rather, He created friends (أولياء) for Himself who grieve (يأسفون) and are pleased (يرضون), and they are created and sustained (مربوبون). So, He made their pleasure His own pleasure and their displeasure His own displeasure. That is because He made them the callers (الدعاة) to Him and the guides (الأدلّاء) to Him. Therefore, they became thus. It is not that this reaches Allah as it reaches His creation, but this is the meaning of what He said concerning that.

And he (peace be upon him) also said: "Whoever insults a friend (وليّاً) of Mine has challenged Me to war (بارزني بالمحاربة) and invited Me to it."

And he (peace be upon him) also said: **مَنْ يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ ** (Whoever obeys the Apostle certainly obeys Allah)

And he (peace be upon him) also said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ (Indeed, those who swear allegiance to you, swear allegiance to Allah) And all of this and its like is according to what I have mentioned to you. And similarly, pleasure (الرضا) and anger (الغضب) and other things that resemble that.

And if grief (الاسف) and weariness (الضجر) were to reach the Originator (المكوّن)—He Who originated (أحدثهما) and created (انشأهما) them—it would be permissible for someone to say that the Originator will perish (يبيد) one day. For if weariness (الضجر) and anger (الغضب) entered Him, change (التغيّر) would enter Him. And if change entered Him, He would not be safe from perishing (الابادة). If that were the case, the Originator (المكوِّن) would not be distinguishable from the originated (المكوَّن), nor the Powerful (القادر) from the subject of power (المقدور), nor the Creator (الخالق) from the created (المخلوق). Highly exalted is Allah above this saying, a great exaltation! He is the Creator of things not out of need (لحاجة). If He is not out of need, then limit (الحدّ) and modality (الكيف) are impossible for Him. Understand that, if Allah wills.

(56) فَجَعَلْناهُمْ سَلَفاً ** (So We made them a precedent) an example (قدوة) for those who came after them among the disbelievers. And it was read سُلُفاً with two ḍammahs. وَمَثَلاً لِلْآخِرِينَ ** (and an example for the latter ones.) and an admonition (عِظة) for them. (57) وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلاً ** (When the son of Mary was cited as an example) referring to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), where it was said that he bore a resemblance to him, behold, your people, Quraysh, مِنْهُ ** (turn away from it) from this example, يَصدُّونَ (turn away). It was said: meaning they clamor (يضجّون) with joy, believing that the Messenger had become bound by it. And it was read with ḍammah (يَصُدُّونَ) from الصدود (aversion), meaning they turn away from the truth and shun it (يعرضون عنه). And it was said that there are two linguistic forms (لغتان) here.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He said concerning this Ayah: الصدود in the Arabic language means laughter (الضّحك).

(٥٨) وَقالُوا أَآلِهَتُنا خَيْرٌ أَمْ هُوَ ** (58) And they said, 'Are our gods better, or is he?' And it was recited with the affirmation (إثبات) of the interrogative hamzah (همزة الاستفهام). ما ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلاً ** They did not cite it to you except for the sake of argument. They did not cite this example except for the sake of argument (الجدل) and contention (الخصومة), not for distinguishing the truth (الحقّ) from falsehood (الباطل). بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ خَصِمُونَ ** Rather they are a contentious lot. They are severe in contention (الخصومة), eager for obstinacy (اللّجاج).

(٥٩) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْناهُ مَثَلاً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ (59) He is but a servant whom We blessed and whom We made an example for the Children of Israel. (٦٠) وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَجَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ مَلائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ ** (60) Had We wished, We would have made angels from among you to be successors on the earth.** (They succeed you on the earth) meaning that Allah is capable of something even more wondrous than that.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Abu Basir, who said: One day, while the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was sitting, the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام approached. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "Indeed, in you there is a resemblance to Jesus son of Mary (عيسى بن مريم). Were it not that factions of my Ummah would say about you what the Christians said about Jesus son of Mary عليه السلام, I would say a word about you such that you would not pass by any gathering of people except that they would take the dust from beneath your feet, seeking blessing (البركة) thereby." He said: So the two Bedouins (الاعرابيان), al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, and a number of Quraysh who were with them became angry and said: "He was not satisfied with setting an example for his cousin except Jesus son of Mary!" So Allah revealed to His Prophet: وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلاً ** (And when the son of Mary was cited as an example) up to His saying: لَجَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ ** (We would have made from among you)—meaning from Banu Hashim (بني هاشم)—مَلائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ ** (angels to be successors on the earth). The hadith continues, and its entirety has already passed in Surat al-Anfal.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Salman al-Farsi رضي الله عنه, who said: While the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was sitting among his companions, he said: "There will enter upon you this hour a man who resembles Jesus son of Mary عليه السلام." So some of those who were sitting with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله went out, hoping to be the one who entered. Then Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام entered. That man said to some of his companions: "Is Muhammad not satisfied with preferring Ali over us until he likens him to Jesus son of Mary? By Allah, our deities (آلِهَتُنا) that we used to worship in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) are better than him!" So Allah revealed in that very gathering: وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلاً إِذا قَوْمُكَ مِنْهُ ** (And when the son of Mary was cited as an example, behold, your people concerning him) يضِجُّون (clamor)—but they distorted it to يَصِدُّونَ وَقالُوا أَآلِهَتُنا خَيْرٌ أَمْ هُوَ ما ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلاً بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ خَصِمُونَ ** (turn away and they said, 'Are our gods better, or is he?' They cite him to you only for the sake of argument. Rather, they are a contentious lot). Indeed, Ali عليه السلام **إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْناهُ مَثَلاً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (is but a servant whom We blessed and whom We made an example for the Children of Israel). Thus, He removed his name from this place.

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, who said: "A man who is the most similar of creation to Jesus will enter through this door." Then Ali entered. So they laughed at this saying, and the verses **وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ** (And when he was cited) were revealed.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, who said: I came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله one day and found Him among a gathering of Quraysh. He looked at me, then said: "O Ali, your likeness in this Ummah is only like the likeness of Jesus son of Mary (عيسى بن مريم). A people loved him and went to excess in their love for him, so they perished; and a people hated him and went to excess in their hatred for him, so they perished; and a people were moderate concerning him, so they were saved." This was grievous to them, and they laughed and said: "He likens him to the Prophets and Messengers!" So this verse was revealed. And in al-Tahdhib, in the supplication (دعاء) of the Day of Ghadir, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "We have responded to Your caller, the Warner and Admonisher, Muhammad, Your servant and Your Messenger, concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام, whom You blessed and whom You made an example for the Children of Israel. Indeed, He is the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, their Master (مولاهم), and their Guardian (وليّهم) until the Day of Resurrection, the Day of Retribution. For You said: إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْناهُ مَثَلاً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ. (He is but a servant whom We blessed and whom We made an example for the Children of Israel.)"

(٦١) وَإِنَّهُ لَعِلْمٌ لِلسَّاعَةِ ** (61) And indeed, that is a sign of the Hour. Al-Qummi: Then He mentioned the significance (خطر) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: وَإِنَّهُ لَعِلْمٌ لِلسَّاعَةِ فَلا تَمْتَرُنَّ بِها وَاتَّبِعُونِ هذا صِراطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ ** (61) And indeed, that is a sign of the Hour. So do not doubt it, and follow Me. (62) This is a straight path. He said: meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And it was said: meaning the descent of Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) is one of the signs (أشراط) of the Hour, by which its proximity is known. فَلا تَمْتَرُنَّ بِها. (61) So do not doubt it.

(٦٢) وَلا يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ الشَّيْطانُ. (62) And let not Satan bar you from it. Al-Qummi (narrated): Meaning the second (Imam) narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (عليه السلام): إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ. (62) Indeed he is your manifest enemy. (٦٣) وَلَمَّا جاءَ عِيسى بِالْبَيِّناتِ قالَ قَدْ جِئْتُكُمْ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَلِأُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي تَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ ** (63) When Jesus came with the manifest proofs, he said, 'I have certainly brought you wisdom, and that I may clarify for you some of that about which you differ. So be wary of Allah and obey me.' regarding what I convey from Him. (٦٤) إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ هذا صِراطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ. (64) Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path. (٦٥) ** فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَحْزابُ ** (65) But the factions differed among themselves. The groups that formed factions مِنْ بَيْنِهِمْ فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (65) So woe to those who did wrong from the factionalists مِنْ عَذابِ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ ** The Resurrection. (65) because of the punishment of a painful day. (٦٦) هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً ** (66) Do they await anything but the Hour, that it should overtake them suddenly suddenly وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** heedless of it. (66) while they are unaware? (٦٧) الْأَخِلَّاءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ. (67) On that day, friends will be enemies of one another, Al-Qummi (narrated): Meaning the friends (الأصدقاء) will be hostile to one another. And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: Beware, every friendship (خلّة) that existed in the world for other than Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will turn into enmity on the Day of Resurrection إِلَّا الْمُتَّقِينَ ** for their friendship, since it was for the sake of Allah, remains beneficial forever and ever. (67) except the Godwary. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah and then said: "By Allah, He did not intend anyone other than you by this." And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "And seek the brotherhood (مؤاخاة) of the pious (الأتقياء), even if it is in the darkness of the earth, and even if you spend your life seeking them. For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has not created anyone better than them on the face of the earth after the Prophets. And Allah, the Exalted, has not bestowed a favor upon a servant like the favor He bestows through the success (التّوفيق) of their companionship." The Almighty Allah said: **الْأَخِلَّاءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقِينَ ** And I believe that whoever seeks a friend without fault in our time will remain without a friend.

(٦٨) **يا عِبادِ لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ وَلا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ ** (O My servants! There shall be no fear come upon you this day, nor shall you grieve) A narration of what the pious ones, who loved one another for the sake of Allah, will be called out with on that Day.

(٦٩) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِآياتِنا. (those who believed in Our signs) Al-Qummi said: meaning the Imams (peace be upon them). وَكانُوا مُسْلِمِينَ. (and were Muslims)

(٧٠) ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ أَنْتُمْ وَأَزْواجُكُمْ ** (Enter paradise, you and your spouses) Meaning: your believing wives. تُحْبَرُونَ. (being felicitated) Al-Qummi said: meaning you will be honored.

(٧١) يُطافُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِصِحافٍ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ وَأَكْوابٍ ** (Platters of gold and cups will be passed around to them) The ṣiḥāf (الصحفة) is the large bowl (القصعة), and the akwāb (الكوب) is a jug (كوز) without a handle. وَفِيها ما تَشْتَهِيهِ الْأَنْفُسُ ** (and therein will be whatever the souls desire) It was also recited: mā tashtahī al-anfus (ما تشتهي الأنفس). وَتَلَذُّ الْأَعْيُنُ ** (and the eyes delight in) By observing it. وَأَنْتُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ ** (and you will remain in it forever) For indeed, every transient bliss necessitates the burden of preservation and the fear of cessation, and is followed by regret in the second state.

In al-Iḥtijāj, narrated from al-Qā’im (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the people of Paradise would procreate when they entered it. He (peace be upon him) replied: "Indeed, there is no pregnancy for women in Paradise, nor childbirth, nor menstruation (طمث), nor post-natal bleeding (نفاس), nor the hardship (شقاء) of childhood. وَفِيها ** (and therein will be) what the الْأَنْفُسُ ** (souls desire) وَتَلَذُّ الْأَعْيُنُ ** (and the eyes delight in), as Allah has said. So, if the believer desires a child, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, creates Him without pregnancy or childbirth, upon the form that He wills, just as He created Adam as a lesson (عبرة)."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: "Indeed, a man in Paradise remains at his dining table for the duration of the days of this world, and he eats in a single meal the equivalent of his eating in this world."

(٧٢) وَتِلْكَ الْجَنَّةُ الَّتِي أُورِثْتُمُوها بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (72) This is the Paradise which you have inherited because of what you used to do. The meaning of inheritance has already been mentioned. (٧٣) لَكُمْ فِيها فاكِهَةٌ كَثِيرَةٌ مِنْها تَأْكُلُونَ ** (73) In it there is abundant fruit for you, of which you will eat. It was said, and perhaps the detailing of the enjoyment through foods and garments, and its repetition in the Quran—even though it is insignificant (حقير) compared to the rest of the bliss of Paradise—is due to the hardship (الشدة) and destitution (الفاقة) that afflicted them. (٧٤) إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ فِي عَذابِ جَهَنَّمَ خالِدُونَ ** (74) Indeed, the guilty will be in the torment of hell, abiding forever. Al-Qummi said: They are the enemies of the Household of Muhammad, the blessings of Allah be upon them (صلوات الله عليهم). (٧٥) لا يُفَتَّرُ عَنْهُمْ ** It will not be lightened for them وَهُمْ فِيهِ مُبْلِسُونَ ** (75) It will not be lightened for them, and they will be despairing in it. Al-Qummi said: meaning, despairing of goodness (الخير). (٧٦) وَما ظَلَمْناهُمْ وَلكِنْ كانُوا هُمُ الظَّالِمِينَ. (76) We did not wrong them, but they were the wrongdoers. (٧٧) وَنادَوْا يا مالِكُ. (77) They will cry out, 'O Malik! In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): that He recited يا مال (Ya Mal) using الترخيم (apocope). It was said, and perhaps this is an indication that due to their weakness, they are unable to pronounce the word completely, and therefore they shortened it when they said: لِيَقْضِ عَلَيْنا رَبُّكَ (Let your Lord finish us off). Meaning: Ask your Lord to finish us off, that is, to cause us to die, derived from قضى عليه (qada 'alayh) when He causes him to die. قالَ إِنَّكُمْ ماكِثُونَ ** (He will say, 'You will stay [here].') There is no escape for you through death or otherwise.

(٧٨) لَقَدْ جِئْناكُمْ بِالْحَقِ ** (78) Certainly We brought you the truth by sending the messengers and sending down the revelation (بالإرسال والانزال). Al-Qummi: It is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). He said: It means by the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ لِلْحَقِّ كارِهُونَ ** but most of you were averse to the truth. He said: meaning, to the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(79) أَمْ أَبْرَمُوا أَمْراً ** (79) Have they settled upon a matter? [This matter is] concerning the denial of the truth (الحقّ) and its rejection, and they did not limit themselves to merely disliking it. Then We are مُبْرِمُونَ ** (settling) a matter concerning their recompense (مجازاتهم).

(80) أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ ** (80) Do they suppose that We do not hear their secret [talk] [meaning] the discourse of their souls (حديث نفسهم) وَنَجْواهُمْ ** [meaning] their private conversation (تناجيهم). بَلى ** (Yes,) We hear it, وَرُسُلُنا ** and Our messengers, meaning the guardian angels (الحفظة), are with them, writing that down.

Al-Qummi said: It means what they covenanted upon in the Ka'bah: that they would not return the matter [of authority] concerning the Household (أهل بيت) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

I say: The explanation of that will come in Surah Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And narrated from Al-Sadiq عليه السلام: This Ayah was revealed concerning them.

(٨١) قُلْ إِنْ كانَ لِلرَّحْمنِ وَلَدٌ فَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْعابِدِينَ ** (Say: 'If the All-beneficent had a child, I would be the first of the worshippers.') And walad (وَلَدٌ) was read with the ḍamma. Al-Qummi said: It means the first of those who disdain (الآنفين) for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), that He should have a child (وَلَدٌ). And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): meaning the deniers (الجاحدين). He said: And the esoteric interpretation (التّأويل) of this saying is contrary (مضادّ) to its apparent meaning (ظاهره).

(٨٢) سُبْحانَ رَبِّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ رَبِّ الْعَرْشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ ** (82) Clear is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, of what they attribute [to Him]. concerning His being one who has a son, for these originated things (المبدعات) are far removed from generating their like (المثل), so what do you think of their Originator (مبدعها) and Creator (خالقها)?

(٨٣) فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا وَيَلْعَبُوا ** (83) So leave them to indulge [in their vain talk] and play in their worldly life حَتَّى يُلاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ ** (83) until they encounter the day they are promised. meaning the Resurrection (القيامة).

(٨٤) **وَهُوَ الَّذِي فِي السَّماءِ إِلهٌ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ إِلهٌ ** (84) It is He who is God in the heaven and God in the earth, deserving to be worshipped in both of them.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who said in a tradition: And His saying: وَهُوَ الَّذِي فِي السَّماءِ إِلهٌ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ إِلهٌ ** (And He is the One who is God in the heaven and God in the earth), and His saying: وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ ** (And He is with you wherever you may be), and His saying: ما يَكُونُ مِنْ نَجْوى ثَلاثَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ رابِعُهُمْ ** (There is no secret counsel of three but He is the fourth of them)—He only intended by that the dominance (استيلاء) of His trustees (امنائه) over all His creation, through the power (القدرة) He installed in them, and that their action is His action. وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْعَلِيمُ.** (84) and He is the All-wise, the All-knowing.

(85) وَتَبارَكَ الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما وَعِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَإِلَيْهِ يَرْجِعُونَ (85) Blessed is He to Whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and with Him is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Him they shall be brought back. And it was read with the tā' (بالتّاء). (86) وَلا يَمْلِكُ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ الشَّفاعَةَ إِلَّا مَنْ شَهِدَ بِالْحَقِّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (86) Those whom they invoke besides Him have no power of intercession, except those who bear witness to the truth and know [the truth]. Al-Qummi said: They are those who were worshipped in this world (الدنيا); they do not possess intercession for those who worshipped them. except those who bear witness to the truth and know [the truth] (meaning) with al-Tawhid (بالتوحيد).

(٨٧) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ ** (And if you ask them, 'Who created them?' they will surely say, 'Allah.') because arrogance (المكابرة) is impossible regarding it due to its extreme clarity. فَأَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ** how then are they turned away? They are turned away from His worship to the worship of other than Him.

(٨٨) وَقِيلِهِ ** (and [He knows] his saying:) meaning the saying of the Messenger, that is, and He knows his saying, or He said his saying. And it was said that the hā' (الهاء) is extra (زائدة). And it was recited with kasrah (بالجرّ) (genitive case), being conjoined (عطفاً) to السَّاعَةِ (the Hour): يا رَبِّ إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ قَوْمٌ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ** (O my Lord! These are indeed a people who do not have faith).

(٨٩) فَاصْفَحْ عَنْهُمْ ** (So disregard them) so turn away from inviting them, despairing of their faith. وَقُلْ سَلامٌ ** (and say, 'Peace!') a farewell from you and a cessation (of interaction). فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (Soon they will know.) a consolation for the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and a threat to them. And it was recited with the tā' (بالتاء).

( A Narrative Discussion )

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Ḥā Mīm al-Zukhruf, Allah will grant him security in his grave from the vermin (هوام) of the earth and the pressure (ضغطة) of the grave until he stands before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Then it (the Surah) will come until it admits him into Paradise by the command of Allah, the Blessed and Exalted."
